# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.
# Category: edge_computing_security

```typescript
import { VercelRequest, VercelResponse } from '@vercel/node';

export default async (req: VercelRequest, res: VercelResponse) => {
  try {
    const config = process.env.EDGE_CONFIG || `API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com'`;
    
    // Split the configuration into key-value pairs
    const configPairs = config.split(",").map(pair => pair.trim().split("="));
    
    // Create an object from the key-value pairs
    let configObj: { [key: string]: string } = {};
    for (let pair of configPairs) {
      const key = pair[0].trim();
      const value = pair[1].replace(/['"]+/g, ''); // remove single or double quotes
      configObj[key] = value;
    }
    
    // Use the configuration object
    console.log(configObj); 
  
    res.status(200).json({ message: "Configuration read successfully" });
  } catch (error) {
    console<｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>res.status(500).json({ error: 'An error occurred while reading the configuration' });
